0: Amh Garanjy could use some help from an adventurer.※ This quest is available for a limited time only.

Amh Garanjy could use some help from an adventurer.
※ This quest is available for a limited time only.

1: Strolling through the Starlight Celebration festivities in Gridania, you encounter Amh Garanjy, enthusiastic Starlight celebrant. The market at the Starlight Stalls is a roaring success─so much so, that the celebrants are too busy to attend to their planned provision of mulled punch. In need of a capable pair of hands, Amh Garanjy entreats your aid in ensuring the warm and festive beverages are available for the festivalgoers. Thankful for your willing assent, she bids you join her at the Starlight Stalls, which you may travel to by speaking with the Starlight Stalls guide at Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre. ※ Please note that you will be unable to complete this quest after the seasonal event has ended. For details, please check the Lodestone.

Strolling through the Starlight Celebration festivities in Gridania, you encounter Amh Garanjy, enthusiastic Starlight celebrant. With grand plans to provide kinderpunsch, a kind of mulled punch, to festivalgoers, Amh Garanjy entreats your aid in the preparations. Thankful for your willing assent, she bids you join her at the Starlight Stalls, which you may travel to by speaking with the Starlight Stalls guide at Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.

※Please note that you will be unable to complete this quest after the seasonal event has ended. For details, please check the Lodestone.

2: You have arrived at the Starlight Stalls, located on Gridania's outskirts, where you catch sight of Amh Garanjy. You make your way over to her to hear further details on the planned punch.※ In the event that you leave the Starlight Stalls, you may return by speaking with the Starlight Stalls guide at Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.

You have arrived at the Starlight Stalls, located on Gridania's outskirts, where you catch sight of Amh Garanjy. You make your way over to her to hear further details on the planned punch.

※In the event that you leave the Starlight Stalls, you may return by speaking with the Starlight Stalls guide at Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.

3: Speaking with Amh Garanjy and Mother Miounne, you learn of the plan to create three separate types of kinderpunsch to represent Gridania, Limsa Lominsa, and Ul'dah─each to be served in an appropriately decorated festive mug. Miounne is still unsure of the best blends to encapsulate the other cities, but there are doubtless some experts on regional cuisine among the visitors to the Gridanian markets. Working your way around the stalls, you keep an eye out for anyone who may be able to furnish you with advice and inspiration for the mulled punch recipes.

Speaking with Amh Garanjy and Mother Miounne, you learn of the plan to create three separate types of kinderpunsch to represent Gridania, Limsa Lominsa, and Ul'dah─each to be served in an appropriately decorated festive mug. Miounne is still unsure of the best blends to encapsulate the other cities, but there are doubtless some experts on regional cuisine among the visitors to the Gridanian markets. You keep an eye out for anyone who may be able to furnish you with advice and inspiration for the mulled punch recipes.

4: You have acquired some useful pointers from several visitors to Gridania, which you are eager to share with Amh Garanjy in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.
You have acquired some useful pointers from several visitors to Gridania. Eager to share your findings with Amh Garanjy, you make your way back to Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.

5: Brimming with inspiration, Amh Garanjy sets about making arrangements to procure the required ingredients. In the meantime, she bids you speak to the children visiting the Starlight Stalls and inform them of the upcoming kinderpunsch on offer. She suggests you take note of any preferences they express, to better recommend a refreshment to their tastes.The Starlight Stalls guide in Mih Khetto’s Amphitheatre will show you to the stalls.

Brimming with inspiration, Amh Garanjy sets about making arrangements to procure the required ingredients. In the meantime, she bids you speak to the children visiting the Starlight Stalls and inform them of the upcoming kinderpunsch on offer. She suggests you take note of any preferences they express, to better recommend a refreshment to their tastes.

※You may travel to the Starlight Stalls by speaking with the guide at Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.

6: With the children suitably informed and enthused, you make your way over to speak to the saturnine Starlight celebrant and assist in serving the kinderpunsch.
The children are now suitably informed and enthused. Your task accomplished, you go to seek out the saturnine Starlight celebrant in order to assist in serving the kinderpunsch.

8: While all the children enjoyed their drinks, it seems that Joultain─a young boy with a strong wanderlust─is particularly replete with festive cheer, thanks to the help of a certain adventurer.If, inspired by the spirit of Starlight, you wish to assist with the punch stall once more, you may speak to the saturnine Starlight celebrant at the Starlight Stalls.

While all the children enjoyed their drinks, it seems that Joultain─a young boy with a strong wanderlust─is particularly replete with festive cheer, thanks to the help of a certain adventurer. If, inspired by the spirit of Starlight, you wish to assist with the punch stall once more, you may speak to the saturnine Starlight celebrant at the Starlight Stalls.

48: Apologies, the stalls are somewhat overcrowded at present. I'd be happy to escort you through shortly, once the situation is in hand.
Apologies, the stalls are overcrowded at present. I'd be happy to show you there once the number of attendees has eased.

49: Happy Starlight, good adventurer! I am Amh Garanjy, Starlight celebrant.Might this be your first time partaking in the festivities?

Happy Starlight, adventurer! I am Amh Garanjy, Starlight celebrant. And you are called <Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, you say? 'Tis a pleasure to make your acquaintance! Might this be your first time partaking in the festivities?

50: Merry Starlight, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! What a pleasure it is to see you again in this most festive of seasons. Allow me to reiterate my thanks for your contributions to Starlights past!

<Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! What a pleasure it is to see you again in this most festive of seasons. Allow me to reiterate my thanks for your contributions to Starlights past!

51: The Starlight Celebration is a festival that finds its origins in an old Ishgardian tale, and is all about granting the wishes of children. And I'm delighted to say that this year's Celebration offers joy and merriment to all, regardless of age, as we once again host our festive market: the Starlight Stalls!

Inspired by an old Ishgardian tale, the Starlight Celebration is about granting the wishes of children. And this year, we are bringing back the festive market that offers joy and merriment to young and old alike: the Starlight Stalls!

52: The Starlight celebrants are pleased to be working with the fine folk at the Carline Canopy to provide kinderpunsch, a kind of mulled punch, to our guests. It was a great success last Starlight—for what could be better than perusing the stalls, warm drink in hand?

The first time around, the Carline Canopy supplied us with kinderpunsch, a kind of mulled punch, which we provided to guests. It proved tremendously popular, so we are planning to provide it again─with a twist!

53: Though we had hoped to try something new for the stalls this Starlight. Alas, while it is a blessing to have so many merrymakers enjoying the market, it means all the celebrants are busy attending to our guests, leaving none available to attend to our plans for the punch.
Alas, with all the celebrants busy attending to the preparation at the stalls, we haven't been able to get working on said twist...

54: And so, I must ask whether I could prevail upon your assistance, my dear adventurer.
If you are not otherwise occupied, might you be willing to lend us a hand?

55: Oh, thank you for your kindness!If I could ask you to join me in the Starlight Stalls, I shall explain the particulars.
I am truly grateful! Without further ado, please join me at the Carline Canopy stall at the market. There, I shall explain the particulars to you.

56: Pray speak to the Starlight Stalls guide, who will gladly escort you.I will see you anon, by the Carline Canopy stall!
When you are ready, the guide yonder will be glad to escort you through. I shall see you anon!

57: Oh, you must be the adventurer who so kindly agreed to help us!Allow me to show you to the Starlight Stalls.
Ah, so you are the adventurer who has agreed to help us! If you would follow me, I shall take you to the Starlight Stalls.

59: We're getting our new lineup ready. Come back soon!
We're currently preparing our new product range. Please come back later!

60: Once again, thank you for your assistance.I sent word to Mother Miounne of the Carline Canopy, and she will be joining us forthwith. Once she arrives, we'll provide all the information you should require.
My thanks again for assisting us. I've asked Miounne to join me in explaining our punch plans. She should be along any moment now.

61: Well now, who have we here? You certainly have an eye for talent, Amh. I take it you're the adventurer she's roped in to help?
Ah, so you're the adventurer who's been roped in to help.

62: And after you did such fine work for the last festival, too.Thanks to you, our kinderpunsch is the talk—and drink—of the town in Starlight season!
An excellent choice, given the fine work you did distributing the punch last festival. It was a favorite among attendees, and the mugs had the desired effect of promoting the Carline Canopy.

63: As Amh Garanjy may have mentioned, the Carline Canopy serves kinderpunsch─a warm, sweet drink made by simmering spices in fruit juice. Last Starlight Celebration, we handed it out in mugs with a Gridanian design.
As Amh may have mentioned, at last year's festival, the Carline Canopy prepared kinderpunsch for the people. This was distributed in mugs with a Gridanian design.

64: The other day, I happened to mention the initiative to my fellow guildmasters. They near jumped at the chance to have Limsa Lominsa and Ul'dah represented, as you can see from our new-and-improved lineup!
When I mentioned this to my guild counterparts in the other city-states, they were keen to have their homes represented on the mugs as well. And so we had these beauties made.

66: The mugs are decorated with local vistas and motifs─a grand ship on the Lominsan mug, and a cactuar on the Ul'dahn mug, for example. And of course, some Starlight decorations have been worked into the designs for a festive touch.
They bear local vistas and motifs, such as a galleon for Limsa Lominsa and a cactuar for Ul'dah, with seasonal flourishes worked in for the festive touch.

68: Since we have such wonderful new mugs, it seems only right to use them to hand out three different types of kinderpunsch!
Since we have now three lovely mugs, we thought it would be fitting to serve up three varieties of kinderpunsch.

69: For the Gridanian punch, we've juiced apples harvested here in the Black Shroud and gently simmered it with complementary flavors including blood currants, herbs and cinnamon.
In Gridania, punch is traditionally made by simmering the juice of locally grown apples with blood currants, cinnamon, and various herbs.

70: Personally, I'm of the mind that we may as well go the full malm with the Lominsan and Ul'dahn punch and create a signature drink from the produce and tastes of the cities. I've just been mulling over what ingredients to use…
For the Lominsan and Ul'dahn varieties, I'm keen to incorporate ingredients found in their respective locales, but I haven't been able to decide on what...

72: Hello! Could we get some mulled punch, please?
(-Lively Youth-)Hello! Could we get some mulled punch, please?

73: Hold on, are those new mug designs!? Woah!

74: Are these other cities!? What are they like? I wanna hear all about them!
(-Lively Youth-)Are the pictures of other cities? I wonder what they're like!

75: Patience, my dear Joultain!We're presently preparing some kinderpunsch that recreates local flavors from these very cities, so if you can but give us a little time, I promise that we'll provide a tour for your tastebuds!
Ah, young Joultain! The punch isn't ready yet, but it shan't be long now. We are currently preparing two new varieties, you see, using ingredients from the cities shown on these mugs.

76: Haha, it seems as though the little ones are looking forward to our enterprise as well. And what a healthy appetite for learning about new lands!
Hehe, it does me well to see the younglings' curiosity for faraway lands.

77: Well then, it falls to us to create Lominsan and Ul'dahn punch that lives up to those high expectations!And I have just the task for you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>.
It further motivates me to serve up kinderpunsch that will not disappoint─which brings me back to the task we have for you.

78: Would you be so kind as to gather information on what ingredients we should use to best capture each city?
We ask that you investigate what ingredients we should use to create punch that best captures each nation.

79: I daresay many visitors from across the realm stop by the shopping district when in Gridania. A seasoned adventurer such as yourself is sure to be able to identify those who hail from Limsa Lominsa and Ul’dah—and to coax out the relevant information!
Gridania's market attracts many visitors from the realm over. An adventurer such as yourself should have no trouble identifying suitable individuals─and coaxing out the relevant information.

80: Marvelous! I will report at once to the other celebrants and keep them apprised of the situation, before returning to my post in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre. I shall be most keen to hear your findings!
Your confidence is most assuring! I shall apprise the other celebrants of the situation, and then return to Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre. I look forward to your findings!

81: Wow, a real-life adventurer…! Thank you, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>. I can't wait to try the kinderpunsch!
So you're an adventurer? That's so amazing!

82: Once again, my thanks for your kind assistance. I look forward to hearing what ingredients the visitors from Limsa Lominsa and Ul’dah suggest!
My thanks again for your kind assistance. I look forward to hearing what ingredients are suggested for our new varieties of kinderpunsch!

83: Mulled punch with the flavors of Limsa, you say?You'll be wanting to throw in plenty of La Noscean oranges, lowland grapes, and lemons.
You want to make punch with a Lominsan twist? Then you'll want to use plenty of La Noscean oranges, lowland grapes, and lemons.

84: Lominsan citrus is renowned far beyond the shores of Vylbrand and keeps scurvy─that bane of sailors─at bay. I ensure we always keep well stocked.
La Noscean citrus is renowned far and wide. It's featured heavily in Lominsan cuisine ever since sailors started using it to ward off scurvy, and I always ensure that our restaurant has ample stock.

85: Oh, did I neglect to mention? Head of procurement at the Bismarck, fine dining establishment in Limsa Lominsa, at your service. If you're looking for quality ingredients at a good price, happy to point you in the right direction!
I'm in charge of procurement at the Bismarck, you see. If you're after quality citrus, I'd be glad to introduce you to our suppliers!

86: If you want to make mulled punch with a Lominsan twist, you can't go wrong with La Noscean citrus!
If you're making punch with a Lominsan twist, I guarantee you can't go wrong with La Noscean citrus!

87: What’s that, you say? Ingredients prized in Ul’dahn cuisine? I could give you a standard answer—nopal, spices—but, oh, what a waste! Our culinary journeys should be an adventure!

What's that? Ingredients that are distinctly Ul'dahn? Well, most of my countrymen would say nopal and spices...but that would be selling our cuisine short, for dining should be an <Emphasis>adventure</Emphasis>!

88: Ohoho! The harmonies of unexpected fusion and avant-garde ingredients… Why, it's a culinary Coliseum, with all the drama of a fierce bout!
The thrill of the unknown... The triumph of audacity... A dish should have all the drama of a championship bout at the Coliseum, oh yes!

89: Ahh, simply sublime. It may be a little…stimulating for mere children, but us adults can appreciate a little experimentation─wouldn't you agree?
Such an experience may be a little stimulating for children, but we grown-ups can appreciate a little excitement. Wouldn't you agree?

90: You want to make a drink to represent Ul'dah?Tea should be a good place to start. Us Ul'danhns can't get through the day without a good, strong brew!
You wish to make a drink that represents Ul'dah? Tea would be a good place to start. We Ul'dahns can't get through the day without a good, strong brew!

91: You'll always find it served up in the drinkeries of Ul'dah, whether you're there for a slap-up meal or just a sweet treat. I've heard tell that the sultana takes hers with the finest butter and honey, you know.
It's served up anywhere you can get a feed─doesn't matter if it's a slap-up meal or just a sweet treat. I've heard tell that the sultana takes hers with generous helpings of butter and honey.

92: Aside from tea, you have Thanalan specialties like vanilla, cloves, black pepper, and pearl ginger. The finest produce will part you from your gil like precious gems.
Aside from tea, Thanalan has specialties like vanilla, cloves, black pepper, and pearl ginger. The finest stuff can cost as much as gemstones.

93: If you're making a drink that represents Ul'dah, it needs to have some tea in it. Butter and spice would make good accents too!
If you're making a drink that represents Ul'dah, it needs to have tea in it. Butter and spice would make good accents too!

94: Welcome back, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Were you able to learn anything promising?

95: But of course! What better to create a signature drink than local produce? I have taken the liberty of noting down the key ingredients, and I believe my fellow celebrants and I should be able to arrange procurement—pray leave it with us.
Oh, those ingredients seem representative of Limsa Lominsa and Ul'dah indeed! We celebrants should be able to procure the items, so pray leave it to us!

96: Once the kinderpunsch is ready, I wonder if you would do the honors and serve the punch to the children? With your intimate knowledge of each city, I suspect it will elevate the experience. You need only speak to my colleague in the Starlight Stalls.
Once the kinderpunsch has been prepared, we would be pleased if you would do the honors and serve it. When it is time, please present yourself to my colleague at the Starlight Stalls.

97: Oh, thank you! In the meantime, could I ask you to speak to the little ones and let them know that the punch will soon be ready? They were so looking forward to it!
And if you happen to have a moment now, might I trouble you to speak with the little ones from earlier?

98: And they seemed ever so keen to learn about cultures beyond Gridania. Perhaps you could listen to where their interests lie, and decide which kind of punch will bring them the most Starlight cheer?
I am hoping that you could learn which city holds the greatest interest for each of them. In so doing, you would be able to recommend varieties of punch that would be the most meaningful.

100: When you let the little ones know that the kinderpunsch is ready, perhaps you could ask them if there’s a specific city that captures their interest. Once you’ve spoken to them all, pray speak to my colleague in the Starlight Stalls!
Please find the little ones and learn what city holds the greatest interest for each of them. And when it is time to distribute the punch, my colleague at the Starlight Stalls will assist you.

101: Don't worry, things are quite in hand here.An enthusiastic young man even offered to help, would you believe!
Rest assured our preparations are proceeding apace. One enthusiastic young man even offered to help.

102: The mulled punch is nearly ready?I'm really looking forward to it!
The punch is almost ready? I can't wait to have some!

103: What do I like? Hmm…fruit! I like apples and berries, but it might be fun to try some fruit that doesn't grow in the Black Shroud.
What do I like to eat? Hmmm...fruits! We have apples and berries here in the wood, but I'd love to try fruits from other places too.

104: They're giving out hot drinks…?That sounds great!
They're giving out hot drinks...? That sounds great!

105: Um… I'm visiting with my mum. It's my first time here, so, I want to learn more about Gridania…!
Um...I'm visiting with my mum. It's my first time in Gridania...so I guess I want to learn more about everything here.

106: Oh, you're the adventurer from before! The mulled punch is almost ready? I'm so there!
Oh, you're the adventurer from before! The mulled punch is almost ready? Hooray!

107: I want to learn more about all the different cities, so I can't decide which type of punch to get… There's no ocean here in Gridania, so one day, I want to get on a big ship and sail off to faraway lands for myself!
I want to learn about all new places, so it's hard to decide on a punch. There's no ocean in Gridania, so it's my dream to go sailing everywhere on a big ship!

108: I'll head over soon! I can't wait to try some exotic fruit!
I'll head over soon! I can't wait to see what kinds of punch there'll be. Something with fruits from other places, I hope!

109: It's my first time in Gridania. Um… I'm looking forward to seeing more of the city!
It's my first time in Gridania...so I guess I want to learn more about everything here.

110: I want to learn more about other lands. One day I'll sail on a big ship and go on an epic journey!
I want to learn about all new places. One day, I'll go sailing everywhere on a big ship!

111: Oh, what good timing. We received the last of the ingredients a moment ago, and have just put the new batches of kinderpunsch on the heat.
Ah, excellent timing. We've received the last of the ingredients and are now brewing the new varieties of kinderpunsch.

112: No doubt we'll have a horde of children descend upon us as soon as we open. By all means, feel free to put the finishing touches to the punch and serve it up yourself—just let me know when you're ready.
We expect a throng of children will descend upon us as soon as we begin serving. Are you ready to attend to them?

115: Review the characteristics of each city and the punch it inspired.
I'd like to review the characteristics of the cities and their punch.

117: Distributing festive cheer will take several minutes. You may wish to cancel any pending Duty Finder registrations.
Serving punch to the younglings will take several minutes. You may wish to cancel any pending Duty Finder registrations.

119: Gridania is in the Black Shroud, a deep forest that the elementals call home. The Carline Canopy kinderpunsch uses apple and cinnamon. You can almost taste the bounty of the forest!
Gridania is located in the Black Shroud, the dominion of the elementals. The Carline Canopy's kinderpunsch uses generous amounts of apple and cinnamon, and is a veritable bounty of the forest.

120: Limsa Lominsa is a port city thronging with ships bringing cuisine and culture from far-flung lands.
Limsa Lominsa is a culinary cradle, where myriad cultures have come together courtesy of trade on the seas.

121: The Lominsan kinderpunsch is made with citrus fruits from La Noscea, including lemons and oranges, in a fusion of flavor that calls to mind the mingling of different peoples in the city itself.
The Lominsan kinderpunsch blends foreign spices with La Noscean citruses, to produce a fusion of flavor that reflects the mingling of peoples in the city.

122: Ul'dah is a sultanate, ruled by Sultana Nanamo Ul Namo. All kinds of exotic goods carried by merchant caravans from faraway lands line the stalls of the bazaar, while the nobility flock to luxurious seating in the Coliseum that gives them a fine view of the clashes between gladiators below.

Ruled by the sultana Nanamo Ul Namo, the nation of Ul'dah thrives on commerce. Its bazaars are lined with exotic wares brought in by caravans, while the Coliseum throngs with nobles who seek the thrill of gladiatorial combat.

123: The Ul'dahn kinderpunsch is a sweet butter tea simmered with dried fruit and black pepper. It combines flavors fit for royalty with just a little hit of spice.
The Ul'dahn kinderpunsch is made by simmering dried fruits and black pepper in a sweet butter tea. The result is a flavor that is royally rich as well as stimulating.

124: Um, I have a friend who used to live in Ul'dah. I want to know what his life was like back there. Do you have any mulled punch that could give me a taste of Ul'dah?
A friend of mine used to live in Ul'dah, and he tells me stories about it. I've always wanted to know what it's like there...if you have something like that?

129: Woah, this tea has a lot of spices, but it's still sweet. This must be what Ul'dah smells like! I'm going to bring my friend over, too!
Wow, this is really good, like a sweet tea with spices! Is this what Ul'dah smells like, I wonder... I'm going to get my friend to come too!

130: This is so good! But what makes it Ul'dah…? I'll go find my friend, and I can ask him about it while we drink punch together!
Wow, this is really good! But...is the flavor really Ul'dahn? I'm going to get my friend and ask him about it!

131: Hello! Do you have a punch with lots of fruit? I'd like to try some fruit I don't usually get to eat round here!
I'd love to see what fruits from other places taste like. Do you have a punch that's made with them?

136: Oh! It's sour, but sweet! This is so good… I didn't realise that La Noscea had so many kinds of fruit that don't grow here in the Black Shroud!
Oh, it's sour, but sweet! I love it! I had no idea they grew so many kinds of citruses in La Noscea.

137: Yum, this punch is great! Come to think of it, I saw the next stall over sells orange juice. Maybe I'll come back and get some in a bit!
This is a bit different, but it's still very nice! There's also a stall nearby selling orange juice, so I'll go and try some later.

140: I heard there's a coliseum somewhere where loads of gladiators fight each other. Bring me some punch that'll make me feel like a mighty fighter of the bloodsands!
I heard there's a place called the Coliseum where gladiators fight each other. I want a punch that'll make me feel like I'm there!

145: The Coliseum is in some place called Ul'dah? Can't say I've heard of it, but the tingling spices are battling it out on my tongue like ferocious gladiators! I'm fired up, in more ways than one!
So the Coliseum is in Ul'dah? I'd love to see a fight there one day...but right now there's a fight in my own mouth! Whew, the spices in this punch sure pack a punch!

146: Oh yeah, these spices are getting me fired up! Time for my gladiator training regime!
Ahhh, the spices in the punch are firing me up! I have to go and train─right now!

147: Oh, you're the nice <If(PlayerParameter(4))>lady<Else/>man</If> from before…!Um… It's my first time here. What do you recommend?
Um...hello again. I've never had mulled punch before. I'm not sure which one I should try...

152: Thank you! Wow, you can really taste the apples…and this mug is so cute! I guess there must be a lot of moogles living in the Black Shroud? Oh, I hope I meet some before I go home…!
Thank you! I love the apple flavor, and the mug is beautiful too! Oh, I hope I can meet some moogles while I'm in Gridania!

153: Thank you! It's so warm, and it tastes great. I'm going to enjoy my trip and explore the rest of the Starlight Stalls!
It's warm and delicious, thank you! It's been really fun here at the stalls. I'm going to keep exploring!

154: Hmm…which should I choose? I want to know more about everywhere…! Let's see… Do you have any punch that will make me feel like I'm on a journey to a faraway land?
Hmmm, what to choose... I suppose I'd like a punch that makes me feel like I'm sailing somewhere far away!

159: Wow, there are so many different flavors! I love it! And this mug is so cool. It's ships like this that connect the whole world…!
Wow, what a great blend of flavors! And I just love the design on the mug. It's ships like this that connect the whole world!

160: Wow, there are so many different flavors! I love it!Come to think of it, traders sell all kinds of goods from other nations in the markets, right? Next time I visit, I'll take a good look!
Wow, what a great blend of flavors! That reminds me, the merchants at the markets also sell wares from faraway places. I should take a closer look at them next time!

161: Thank you, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>! This was a lot of fun!
Thank you, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>! I've had a lot of fun!

162: Looks like the children really enjoyed your choice of punch. A job well done, I suppose.
It seems the children really enjoyed their punch. You did well indeed with your selections.

163: How lovely to see them enjoying the punch…mostly. Well, it looks like all the children who wanted some have had their fill for now.
Well, that appears to be all of the children for now. Our thanks for helping them to enjoy their punch.

164: I'm sure I can manage from here—how about you head back to Amh Garanjy and let her know how the inaugural punch provisioning went? Don't worry, I'll make sure everyone has as much punch as they could possibly want, whether guest or celebrant.
From hereon, we celebrants will tend to the punch distribution with your fine example as a guide. You need only report back to Amh Garanjy─she will be eager to know how we fare.

165: Knowing Amh Garanjy, I'm sure she's fretting about how it's all going.You can leave the stall to me and let her know!
Please leave the rest of the distribution to us, and report back to Amh Garanjy. She will be eager to know how we fare.

166: Oh, here you are. I've just finished putting out a few fires at the Adventurers' Guild, so I thought I'd stop by and see how you were getting on.
I've just finished putting out a few proverbial fires at the guild, so I thought I'd come by and see how you were getting on.

167: The more the merrier! And if you're here, my friend, I can only assume that the kinderpunsch has been handed out to the little ones as planned?
The more the merrier, as I always say! And if you're here, my friend, may I assume that the kinderpunsch has been handed out to the little ones as planned?

168: Wonderful! Once again, you have my thanks. I do hope that the Starlight Stalls have given the dear children a glimpse into a wider world they may one day get to explore for themselves!
We are truly in your debt. With your help, we have not only brought the children festive cheer, but also expanded their horizons.

169: And if they come back to try all three types of punch, they'll spend even longer in the stalls and leave having enjoyed themselves all the more─which is what we want.
Of course, they're all more than welcome to return and try the other types of punch. The longer they enjoy themselves at the stalls, the better.

170: Good, I found you! I wanted to say thank you again─to Miss Garanjy and Mother Miounne, too!
There you are, adventurer! I wanted to thank you again─and Miss Garanjy and Miounne too!

171: Oh, you are most welcome! I take it you enjoyed the mulled punch?
It was absolutely our pleasure. If you enjoyed the punch, then nothing would please us more.

172: When I grow up, I want to be someone who knows all about faraway lands─just like you, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>! I'll go and see what’s out there for myself, and tell everyone all about it.

When I grow up, I want to be someone who knows lots about the world like you, <If(PlayerParameter(4))>miss<Else/>mister</If>! I'll go and see what's out there for myself, and tell everyone all about it.

173: And I want to tell everyone about Gridania, too. The Starlight Stalls have reminded me just how much I love it here!
And I want to tell everyone about Gridania too. The Starlight Stalls have reminded me just how much I love it here!

174: Oh, what a lovely Starlight wish! I've no doubt the Spinner herself will have heard your words, little one.
That is a wonderful wish, and I've no doubt that the Spinner Herself has heard it!

175: I can already tell that I'll be seeing you stride into my Carline Canopy to register with the guild one day, young man. And I'll look forward to hearing your tales of courage and high adventure.
When the time comes, it'll be my pleasure to register you as an adventurer, young man. And I'll look forward to hearing your tales of courage and high adventure.

176: Of course, that goes for you as well, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. I always have time for your stories.
That goes for you as well, of course.

177: The bright smile on young Joultain's face was all thanks to you, my friend. It seems you've truly inspired him!The kinderpunsch stall will continue to serve its festive cheer to one and all. Should you be able to spare the time away from your adventures, we would gladly welcome your aid again!
It seems you've truly inspired young Joultain. And should you still have time to spare, you could potentially inspire even more souls─by helping us serve up more kinderpunsch!

178: The Starlight Stalls guide will be happy to show you to the stalls whenever you so wish, until the festivities come to a close.I do hope you'll find the time to peruse the market yourself. Once again, a very Merry Starlight to you, my friend!

We'd also be glad if you simply enjoyed the festivities hereon─it is entirely up to you. While the celebration continues, our guide will be pleased to show you to the stalls. My thanks again, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, and happy Starlight!

180: You can speak to the saturnine Starlight celebrant at the Starlight Stalls if you want to lend a hand by serving more kinderpunsch.
You can speak to the saturnine Starlight celebrant at the Starlight Stalls if you wish to help serve more kinderpunsch.

181: Who knows what familiar faces you might encounter…?
In so doing, you may well encounter some familiar faces!
